input.description: Find new game invitation sending code
input.prompt:
In this Next.js repo (/Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy), find the code behind the "New game" admin page at src/app/footy/admin/newgame (or similar), which has a checkbox "Override time check (normally invitations are sent 9am the working day before the game)", a "Custom message" textarea, and a "Send invitations" button that should eventually show "Invitations ready" / "Invitations can be sent now." text.

I need:
1. The path and content of the page/component rendering this form (checkbox, custom message field, Send invitations button).
2. The path and content of the server action it calls on submit.
3. The path and content of the "core" business logic function in src/lib/core/ that handles sending/scheduling invitations, especially any logic that checks the current time against 9am the working day before the game, and how/whether it respects an "override" flag.
4. Any place where "Invitations ready" or "Invitations can be sent now" text is rendered.

Report back the relevant file paths and the exact code (with line numbers) for the override-check logic, so I can determine why ticking the override checkbox doesn't cause invitations to be sent immediately (they seem to be silently skipped instead).

content.text: I'm exploring the codebase to find the new-game invitation form and the logic behind the override time check.

input.command: grep -rn "Invitations ready\|Invitations can be sent" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" src/ | grep -v ".vitest.spec"

src/lib/invitations.ts

1	import z from 'zod';
2	
3	import type { InvitationDecision } from '@/lib/invitations';
4	
5	export const NewGameInputSchema = z.object({
6	    overrideTimeCheck: z.boolean(),
7	    customMessage: z.string().max(5000).optional(),
8	});
9	
10	export type NewGameInput = z.infer<typeof NewGameInputSchema>;
11	
12	/**
13	 * Server action proxy type for the triggerInvitations action.
14	 * Enables dependency injection for components and stories without importing
15	 * the server-only action directly.
16	 *
17	 * Triggers the process of sending game invitations based on the provided input,
18	 * checking timing constraints and game availability before sending.
19	 *
20	 * @param data - Validated input containing optional custom message and override time check flag.
21	 * @returns A promise that resolves to an InvitationDecision indicating the status and
22	 * associated game day ID if applicable.
23	 */
24	export type TriggerInvitationsProxy = (data: NewGameInput) => Promise<InvitationDecision>;
25

input.description: Check newgame.spec.ts change in the mount-loading-state commit

content.text: This is the same class of flaky-click bug that was already fixed in `GameInvitationResponseForm` (commit `7e947051`, "Set loading state for submit button... until mounted — fixes flaky Playwright test") but was never applied to `NewGameForm`, per that commit's own note "probably should be extended to all forms." The "Send invitations" button has no `useMounted()` guard, so a click can land before React hydration attaches the submit handler — nothing happens, and the checkbox/message stay as typed since no navigation occurs. I'll apply the same fix here.

input.description: Show diff stat of changes

result.output:
e2e/newgame.spec.ts                        | 1 +
 src/components/NewGameForm/NewGameForm.tsx | 4 +++-

content.text:
Typecheck, lint, and knip all pass clean, and the e2e test now passes reliably.

**Root cause:** `NewGameForm`'s "Send invitations" button had no hydration guard, so a Playwright click could land before React attached the submit handler — the click was a no-op (nothing thrown, no navigation, form values untouched), which is exactly what the stale report showed: checkbox still ticked, message still filled, but no notification ever appeared. This is the identical bug that commit `7e947051` ("Set loading state for submit button in New Game form until mounted") already fixed for `GameInvitationResponseForm`'s "Save Response" button — that commit's own message noted it "probably should be extended to all forms," but `NewGameForm` was never updated.

**Fix applied** (mirroring the existing pattern):
- `src/components/NewGameForm/NewGameForm.tsx`: added `useMounted()` from `@mantine/hooks` and `loading={!mounted}` on the submit button.
- `e2e/newgame.spec.ts`: wait for the button to be enabled before clicking, same as the `Save Response` button's test.

Verified by rebuilding and running `e2e/newgame.spec.ts` against chromium — all 3 tests pass.
